# UC-10.12.8 · SOX Access Control Audit

## Description

Evidence of periodic access reviews and segregation of duties for financially relevant systems.

## Value

Evidence of periodic access reviews and segregation of duties for financially relevant systems.

## Implementation

Map SoD rules; quarterly certification of role membership; alert on emergency access account usage without ticket.

## Detailed Implementation

Prerequisites
• Install and configure the required add-on or app: Active Directory, ERP (SAP/Oracle) TA.
• Ensure the following data sources are available: `sourcetype="WinEventLog:Security"` (AD group changes), `sourcetype="sap:audit"` / `sourcetype="oracle:audit"` (ERP journal postings).
• For app installation, inputs.conf, and Splunk directory layout, see the Implementation guide: docs/implementation-guide.md

Step 1 — Configure data collection
Map SoD rules; quarterly certification of role membership; alert on emergency access account usage without ticket.

Step 2 — Create the search and alert
Run the following SPL in Search (then save as report or alert; adjust time range and threshold as needed):

```spl
index=windows sourcetype="WinEventLog:Security" EventCode IN (4720,4728,4732)
| lookup sox_sensitive_roles SubjectUserName as user OUTPUT role owner
| stats latest(_time) as last_use, values(EventCode) as ad_events by user, role
| where isnotnull(role)
```

Understanding this SPL

**SOX Access Control Audit** — Evidence of periodic access reviews and segregation of duties for financially relevant systems.

Documented **Data sources**: `sourcetype="WinEventLog:Security"` (AD group changes), `sourcetype="sap:audit"` / `sourcetype="oracle:audit"` (ERP journal postings). **App/TA** (typical add-on context): Active Directory, ERP (SAP/Oracle) TA. The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed—rename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

The first pipeline stage scopes events using **index**: windows; **sourcetype**: WinEventLog:Security. That sourcetype matches what this use case lists under Data sources.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

• Scopes the data: index=windows, sourcetype="WinEventLog:Security". Cross-check against **Data sources** above so indexes and sourcetypes match your ingestion.
• Enriches events using `lookup` (lookup definition + optional OUTPUT fields).
• `stats` rolls up events into metrics; results are split **by user, role** so each row reflects one combination of those dimensions.
• Filters the current rows with `where isnotnull(role)` — typically the threshold or rule expression for this monitoring goal.


Step 3 — Validate
Run the search on recent production data, spot-check a handful of results against the source system or operational runbooks (cash operations, EHR, payment gateway, etc.), and adjust thresholds to match your seasonal patterns and test windows so teams are not flooded with noise.

Step 4 — Operationalize
Add the search to a dashboard or set up alert actions (email, webhook, PagerDuty, etc.) as required. Document the use case in your runbook and assign an owner. Consider visualizations: Table (SoD violations), Bar chart (access by application), Timeline (privileged use).

## SPL

```spl
index=windows sourcetype="WinEventLog:Security" EventCode IN (4720,4728,4732)
| lookup sox_sensitive_roles SubjectUserName as user OUTPUT role owner
| stats latest(_time) as last_use, values(EventCode) as ad_events by user, role
| where isnotnull(role)
```

## Visualization

Table (SoD violations), Bar chart (access by application), Timeline (privileged use).
